data.text: The termination logic itself looks correct. But note `providerChildStatus` (which sets the "terminated by running-phase guard" diagnostic) is called by BOTH `scanProviderChildrenForStatus` (status-only, no kill) and `runningProviderChildGuard` (actually kills). Let me trace who calls the status scanner.
Found it. `execute_bead_loop.go:1542` calls `scanProviderChildrenForStatus` which produces the "terminated by running-phase guard" diagnostic without killing anything. Let me look at that context.

52	// TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue covers watch mode: the loop must
53	// NOT exit when nextCandidate returns no
54	// eligible bead. It must reset the per-Run attempted/hookFailed maps and
55	// sleep, then poll again. Cancelling the context is the only way out.
56	func TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue(t *testing.T) {
57		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
58		require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))
59	
60		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
61			Store: store,
62			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {

71		// Use a tiny poll interval so the test runs quickly; cancel after enough
72		// wall-clock for several empty-poll cycles to confirm the loop is still
73		// running and not bailing out after the first empty poll.
74		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
75		pollInterval := 5 * time.Millisecond
76		cancelAfter := 50 * time.Millisecond
77		go func() {
78			time.Sleep(cancelAfter)
79			cancel()
80		}()
81	
82		start := time.Now()
83		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
84			Mode:         executeloop.ModeWatch,
85			IdleInterval: pollInterval,
86		})
87		elapsed := time.Since(start)
88	
89		// Context cancellation surfaces as the returned error. The key
90		// invariant: the loop survived past the first empty poll.
91		require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
92		require.NotNil(t, result)
93		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
94		assert.True(t, elapsed >= cancelAfter,
95			"loop must run until ctx cancellation; ran for %s, expected >= %s", elapsed, cancelAfter)
96	}

203			"ddx-preclaim-warn-5",
204		}

211		}
212	
213		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{

219					ResultRev: "rev-" + beadID,
220				}, nil
221			}),
222		}
223	
224		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
225		defer cancel()
226	
227		var eventSink, logBuf bytes.Buffer
228		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
229		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
230		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
231			Mode:                        executeloop.ModeDrain,
232			Log:                         &logBuf,
233			EventSink:                   &eventSink,
234			NoReview:                    true,
235			PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold: DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold,
236			PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
237				return PreClaimIntakeResult{
238					Outcome: PreClaimIntakeError,
239					Reason:  "system_unready",
240					Detail:  "shared readiness schema mismatch",
241				}, nil
242			},
243		})
244	
245		require.NoError(t, err)
246		require.NotNil(t, result)
247		assert.Equal(t, len(beadIDs)-1, result.Attempts)
248		assert.Equal(t, len(beadIDs)-1, result.Successes)
249		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Failures)
250		require.NotNil(t, result.OperatorAttention)
251		assert.Equal(t, "preclaim_warn_repeated", result.OperatorAttention.Reason)
252		assert.Equal(t, beadIDs[len(beadIDs)-1], result.OperatorAttention.BeadID)
253		assert.Equal(t, "operator_attention", result.ExitReason)
254		assert.Equal(t, "OperatorAttention", result.StopCondition)
255	
256		for _, beadID := range beadIDs[:len(beadIDs)-1] {
257			got, err := store.Get(context.Background(), beadID)
258			require.NoError(t, err)
259			assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status)
260			assert.Nil(t, got.Extra["work-retry-after"])
261		}
262		lastBead, err := store.Get(context.Background(), beadIDs[len(beadIDs)-1])
263		require.NoError(t, err)
264		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, lastBead.Status)
265	
266		lines := strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(eventSink.String()), "\n")
267		var escalations []map[string]any
268		for _, line := range lines {
269			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
270				continue
271			}
272			var entry map[string]any
273			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry))
274			if entry["type"] != "loop.operator_attention" {
275				continue
276			}
277			data, ok := entry["data"].(map[string]any)
278			require.True(t, ok)
279			if data["reason"] == "preclaim_warn_repeated" {
280				escalations = append(escalations, data)
281			}
282		}
283		require.Len(t, escalations, 1, "exactly one repeated-warn escalation is expected")
284		esc := escalations[0]
285		assert.NotEmpty(t, esc["fingerprint"])
286		assert.Equal(t, float64(DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold), esc["count"])
287		distinctIDs, ok := esc["distinct_bead_ids"].([]any)
288		require.True(t, ok, "distinct_bead_ids must be a JSON array")
289		assert.Len(t, distinctIDs, DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold)
290		assert.Equal(t, "preclaim_warn_repeated", esc["reason"])
291		assert.Equal(t, "shared readiness schema mismatch", esc["example_detail"])
292		assert.NotEmpty(t, esc["example_payload"])
293	
294		lastEvents, err := store.Events(beadIDs[len(beadIDs)-1])
295		require.NoError(t, err)
296		var operatorAttentionEvent *bead.BeadEvent
297		for i := range lastEvents {
298			if lastEvents[i].Kind == "operator_attention" && lastEvents[i].Summary == "preclaim_warn_repeated" {
299				operatorAttentionEvent = &lastEvents[i]
300				break
301			}
302		}
303		require.NotNil(t, operatorAttentionEvent, "a durable operator_attention event must be recorded")
304		var body map[string]any
305		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(operatorAttentionEvent.Body), &body))
306		assert.Equal(t, "preclaim_warn_repeated", body["reason"])
307		assert.Equal(t, "shared readiness schema mismatch", body["example_detail"])
308		assert.Equal(t, float64(DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold), body["count"])
309		assert.NotEmpty(t, body["fingerprint"])
310	}

432	// TestWorkWatch_PreClaimIdleEscalatesAfterRepeatedSameDetail covers
433	// ddx-df77e668 AC #3: when the worker idles on the same pre-claim blocker for
434	// preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold consecutive cycles, it emits a non-terminal
435	// loop.operator_attention event carrying the bead-id, detail, and elapsed-idle
436	// instead of looping silently. The beads are never cooldowned, and the loop
437	// keeps idling (the escalation does not stop the worker).
438	func TestWorkWatch_PreClaimIdleEscalatesAfterRepeatedSameDetail(t *testing.T) {
439		store, first, second := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
440		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
441			Store: store,
442			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
443				t.Errorf("executor must not run while pre-claim idles")
444				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
445			}),
446		}
447	
448		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
449		defer cancel()
450		progressCh := make(chan ProgressEvent, 256)
451		done := make(chan struct{})
452		var idleCount int32
453		go func() {
454			for {
455				select {
456				case evt := <-progressCh:
457					if evt.Phase == "loop.idle" && evt.Message == preClaimIdleReasonTrackerContention {
458						if atomic.AddInt32(&idleCount, 1) >= int32(preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold)+2 {
459							cancel()
460							return
461						}
462					}
463				case <-done:
464					return
465				}
466			}
467		}()
468		defer close(done)
469	
470		var eventSink, logBuf bytes.Buffer
471		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
472		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
473		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
474			Mode:         executeloop.ModeWatch,
475			IdleInterval: time.Millisecond,
476			Log:          &logBuf,
477			EventSink:    &eventSink,
478			ProgressCh:   progressCh,
479			SessionID:    "sess-escalate",
480			WorkerID:     "worker-escalate",
481			PreClaimHook: func(ctx context.Context) error {
482				return errors.New("landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s:\nM\t.ddx/beads.jsonl\nM\t.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl")
483			},
484		})
485	
486		require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
487		require.NotNil(t, result)
488		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
489		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Failures)
490		// The escalation is non-terminal: it does not populate a terminal stop.
491		assert.Nil(t, result.OperatorAttention)
492	
493		// Beads stay open and uncooldowned — tracker contention never faults a bead.
494		gotFirst, err := store.Get(context.Background(), first.ID)
495		require.NoError(t, err)
496		gotSecond, err := store.Get(context.Background(), second.ID)
497		require.NoError(t, err)
498		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, gotFirst.Status)
499		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, gotSecond.Status)
500		assert.Nil(t, gotFirst.Extra["work-retry-after"])
501	
502		// Exactly one operator-attention escalation event is emitted for the streak.
503		lines := strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(eventSink.String()), "\n")
504		var escalations []map[string]any
505		for _, line := range lines {
506			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
507				continue
508			}
509			var entry map[string]any
510			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry))
511			if entry["type"] != "loop.operator_attention" {
512				continue
513			}
514			data, ok := entry["data"].(map[string]any)
515			require.True(t, ok)
516			if data["reason"] == "preclaim_idle_escalation" {
517				escalations = append(escalations, data)
518			}
519		}
520		require.Len(t, escalations, 1, "exactly one escalation event per same-detail streak")
521		esc := escalations[0]
522		assert.Contains(t, []any{first.ID, second.ID}, esc["bead_id"], "escalation must name a skipped bead")
523		assert.Contains(t, esc["detail"], ".ddx/beads.jsonl", "escalation must carry the blocker detail")
524		elapsed, ok := esc["elapsed_idle"].(string)
525		require.True(t, ok, "escalation must carry elapsed_idle")
526		assert.NotEmpty(t, elapsed)
527		idleCountVal, ok := esc["idle_count"].(float64)
528		require.True(t, ok)
529		assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, int(idleCountVal), preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold)
530	
531		assert.Contains(t, logBuf.String(), "operator attention: pre-claim idled")
532	}

534	// TestExecuteBeadLoop_ClaimSuccessRateWarnsBelowThreshold verifies the
535	// rolling claim-success signal: a full window of non-successful claim attempts
536	// emits exactly one operator-attention warning, a later successful claim
537	// clears the warned state, and the next below-threshold crossing warns again.
538	func TestExecuteBeadLoop_ClaimSuccessRateWarnsBelowThreshold(t *testing.T) {
539		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
540		require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))
541		for i := 1; i <= 3; i++ {
542			require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), &bead.Bead{
543				ID:       fmt.Sprintf("ddx-claim-rate-%d", i),
544				Title:    fmt.Sprintf("claim rate bead %d", i),
545				Priority: i,
546			}))
547		}
548	
549		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
550		defer cancel()
551	
552		workerStore := &claimSuccessRateWarningStore{Store: store, cancel: cancel}
553		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
554			Store: workerStore,
555			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
556				return ExecuteBeadReport{
557					BeadID:    beadID,
558					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
559					ResultRev: "rev-" + beadID,
560				}, nil
561			}),
562		}
563	
564		var logBuf, eventSink bytes.Buffer
565		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
566		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
567		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
568			Mode:                      executeloop.ModeDrain,
569			Log:                       &logBuf,
570			EventSink:                 &eventSink,
571			WorkerID:                  "worker-claim-rate",
572			SessionID:                 "sess-claim-rate",
573			ClaimSuccessRateWindow:    3,
574			ClaimSuccessRateThreshold: 0.5,
575		})
576	
577		require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
578		require.NotNil(t, result)
579		assert.Equal(t, 2, strings.Count(logBuf.String(), "claim success rate"), "warning should emit once per below-threshold crossing")
580	
581		lines := strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(eventSink.String()), "\n")
582		var warnings []map[string]any
583		for _, line := range lines {
584			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
585				continue
586			}
587			var entry map[string]any
588			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry))
589			if entry["type"] != "loop.operator_attention" {
590				continue
591			}
592			data, ok := entry["data"].(map[string]any)
593			require.True(t, ok)
594			if data["reason"] != "claim_success_rate_below_threshold" {
595				continue
596			}
597			warnings = append(warnings, data)
598		}
599	
600		require.Len(t, warnings, 2, "a later success must clear the warned state so a new crossing warns again")
601		assert.Equal(t, float64(3), warnings[0]["window_size"])
602		assert.Equal(t, float64(0.5), warnings[0]["threshold"])
603		assert.Equal(t, float64(0), warnings[0]["successes"])
604		assert.Equal(t, float64(3), warnings[0]["non_successes"])
605		assert.Equal(t, float64(3), warnings[1]["window_size"])
606		assert.Equal(t, float64(0.5), warnings[1]["threshold"])
607		assert.Equal(t, float64(1), warnings[1]["successes"])
608		assert.Equal(t, float64(2), warnings[1]["non_successes"])
609	}

611	func TestWorkLoop_PreDispatchDirtyImplementationPreservesAndContinues(t *testing.T) {
612		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
613		require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))
614		first := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-preserve-0001", Title: "Preserve dirty root", IssueType: "task"}
615		require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), first))
616	
617		projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
618		dirtyRel := filepath.Join("cli", "internal", "agent", "dirty_impl.go")
619		dirtyPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, dirtyRel)
620		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dirtyPath), 0o755))
621		dirtyContent := "package agent\n"
622		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(dirtyPath, []byte(dirtyContent), 0o644))
623	
624		var execCalls int32
625		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
626			Store: store,
627			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
628				call := atomic.AddInt32(&execCalls, 1)
629				if call == 1 {
630					return ExecuteBeadReport{}, fmt.Errorf(
631						"%s%s; commit or clean those files before rerunning so the bead's [ddx-<id>] substantive commit stays intentional",
632						preExecuteCheckpointDirtyMarker,
633						filepath.ToSlash(dirtyRel),
634					)
635				}
636				require.Empty(t, runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "status", "--short", "--", filepath.ToSlash(dirtyRel)),
637					"preserved dirty path must be restored to HEAD before the next dispatch")
638				_, showErr := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "show", "HEAD:"+filepath.ToSlash(dirtyRel))
639				require.Error(t, showErr, "the dirty implementation file must not be folded into HEAD")
640				return ExecuteBeadReport{
641					BeadID:    beadID,
642					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
643					Detail:    "merged cleanly",
644					SessionID: "sess-preserved",
645					ResultRev: "c0ffee",
646				}, nil
647			}),
648		}
649	
650		var logBuf, eventSink bytes.Buffer
651		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
652		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
653		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
654		defer cancel()
655		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
656			Mode:         executeloop.ModeWatch,
657			IdleInterval: time.Hour,
658			Log:          &logBuf,
659			WakeCh:       make(chan struct{}),
660			EventSink:    &eventSink,
661			ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
662			SessionID:    "sess-preserve-watch",
663			WorkerID:     "worker-preserve-watch",
664		})
665	
666		if err != nil {
667			require.True(t,
668				errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) || errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded),
669				"worker.Run should return context.Canceled or context.DeadlineExceeded, got %v", err,
670			)
671		}
672		require.NotNil(t, result)
673		assert.Nil(t, result.OperatorAttention)
674		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
675		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
676		assert.Equal(t, int32(2), atomic.LoadInt32(&execCalls), "watch mode must redispatch after preserving root dirt")
677	
678		gotFirst, err := store.Get(context.Background(), first.ID)
679		require.NoError(t, err)
680		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, gotFirst.Status)
681	
682		lines := strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(eventSink.String()), "\n")
683		var preserved map[string]any
684		for _, line := range lines {
685			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
686				continue
687			}
688			var entry map[string]any
689			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry))
690			if entry["type"] != "loop.pre_dispatch_dirty_preserved" {
691				continue
692			}
693			data, ok := entry["data"].(map[string]any)
694			require.True(t, ok, "loop.pre_dispatch_dirty_preserved must include data")
695			preserved = data
696			break
697		}
698		require.NotNil(t, preserved, "watch mode must emit a preservation event instead of operator attention")
699		assert.Equal(t, first.ID, preserved["bead_id"])
700		assert.Equal(t, projectRoot, preserved["project_root"])
701		require.Len(t, preserved["dirty_paths"], 1)
702		assert.Contains(t, preserved["dirty_paths"], filepath.ToSlash(dirtyRel))
703		preserveRef, ok := preserved["preserve_ref"].(string)
704		require.True(t, ok)
705		require.NotEmpty(t, preserveRef)
706		recoverCommand, ok := preserved["recover_command"].(string)
707		require.True(t, ok)
708		assert.Equal(t, "git stash apply "+preserveRef, recoverCommand)
709		assert.NotEmpty(t, runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "rev-parse", preserveRef))
710	
711		out := logBuf.String()
712		assert.Contains(t, out, preserveRef)
713		assert.Contains(t, out, filepath.ToSlash(dirtyRel))
714		assert.Contains(t, out, recoverCommand)
715		assert.Empty(t, runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "status", "--short", "--", filepath.ToSlash(dirtyRel)))
716	
717		applyOut, applyErr := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "stash", "apply", preserveRef)
718		require.NoError(t, applyErr, applyOut)
719		assert.Contains(t, runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "status", "--short", "--", filepath.ToSlash(dirtyRel)), "?? "+filepath.ToSlash(dirtyRel))
720	}

722	func TestWorkWatchDoesNotPreserveJustLandedPathsBeforeNextClaim(t *testing.T) {
723		if testing.Short() {
724			t.Skip("watch timing is race-sensitive under -short; covered by the full suite")
725		}
726	
727		projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 2)
728		store := bead.NewStore(ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot))
729	
730		trackedRel := filepath.Join("cli", "cmd", "run_test_helpers.go")
731		trackedPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, trackedRel)
732		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(trackedPath), 0o755))
733		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(trackedPath, []byte("package cmd\n"), 0o644))
734		runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "add", trackedRel)
735		runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "commit", "-m", "test: seed tracked helper")
736	
737		dirFile := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")
738		writeDirectiveFile(t, dirFile, []string{
739			`run if [ "$DDX_BEAD_ID" = "ddx-int-0001" ]; then mv cli/cmd/run_test_helpers.go cli/cmd/run_test_helpers_renamed.go; else printf 'package cmd\n' > cli/cmd/second_dispatch.go; fi`,
740			`commit test: ${DDX_BEAD_ID}`,
741		})
742	
743		originalLister := preDispatchDirtyPathLister
744		t.Cleanup(func() {
745			preDispatchDirtyPathLister = originalLister
746		})
747		var callCount atomic.Int32
748		preDispatchDirtyPathLister = func(root string) ([]string, error) {
749			require.Equal(t, projectRoot, root)
750			switch callCount.Add(1) {
751			case 1:
752				return nil, nil
753			case 2:
754				return []string{
755					filepath.ToSlash(trackedRel),
756					"cli/cmd/run_test_helpers_renamed.go",
757				}, nil
758			case 3:
759				return nil, nil
760			default:
761				return nil, nil
762			}
763		}
764	
765		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
766			Store:    store,
767			Executor: scriptHarnessExecutor(t, projectRoot, dirFile),
768		}
769	
770		var eventSink bytes.Buffer
771		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
772		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
773		// Use a generous outer ceiling (safety net) and rely on synchronization
774		// (polling for both beads to close) to cancel the worker once the
775		// behavior under test has happened. The earlier 750ms wall-clock budget
776		// was too tight for two real git claim+execute+land cycles on a
777		// normally-loaded developer machine (observed 1.9s-7s elapsed). The
778		// surrounding tests use the same require.Eventually idiom -- see
779		// internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go.
780		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
781		defer cancel()
782	
783		type runOutcome struct {
784			result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult
785			err    error
786		}
787		runCh := make(chan runOutcome, 1)
788		go func() {
789			result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
790				Mode:         executeloop.ModeWatch,
791				IdleInterval: time.Hour,
792				EventSink:    &eventSink,
793				ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
794				SessionID:    "sess-watch-stable-predispatch",
795				WorkerID:     "worker-watch-stable-predispatch",
796			})
797			runCh <- runOutcome{result: result, err: err}
798		}()
799	
800		// Wait deterministically for both beads to close (the behavior under
801		// test), then cancel the worker so Run returns context.Canceled.
802		require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
803			first, err := store.Get(context.Background(), "ddx-int-0001")
804			if err != nil || first.Status != bead.StatusClosed {
805				return false
806			}
807			second, err := store.Get(context.Background(), "ddx-int-0002")
808			if err != nil || second.Status != bead.StatusClosed {
809				return false
810			}
811			return true
812		}, 25*time.Second, 10*time.Millisecond, "both beads must close within the safety net")
813	
814		cancel()
815		outcome := <-runCh
816		result, err := outcome.result, outcome.err
817	
818		require.Error(t, err)
819		require.True(t,
820			errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) || errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded),
821			"worker.Run should return context.Canceled or context.DeadlineExceeded, got %v", err)
822		require.NotNil(t, result)
823		assert.Equal(t, 2, result.Attempts)
824		assert.Equal(t, 2, result.Successes)
825		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Failures)
826		assert.Nil(t, result.OperatorAttention)
827		assert.NotContains(t, eventSink.String(), `"type":"loop.pre_dispatch_dirty_preserved"`)
828	
829		first, getErr := store.Get(context.Background(), "ddx-int-0001")
830		require.NoError(t, getErr)
831		second, getErr := store.Get(context.Background(), "ddx-int-0002")
832		require.NoError(t, getErr)
833		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, first.Status)
834		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, second.Status)
835	
836		refsOut, refsErr := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "for-each-ref", "--format=%(refname)", "refs/ddx/pre-dispatch")
837		require.NoError(t, refsErr)
838		assert.Empty(t, strings.TrimSpace(refsOut))
839	}

1	package agent
2	
3	// dispatch.go owns the SD-024 dispatch seam shared by execute-bead and the
4	// post-merge reviewer. It threads durable knobs from a sealed
5	// config.ResolvedConfig with per-invocation plumbing/intent from an
6	// AgentRunRuntime through the same service-or-runner routing used by

8

14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
15		agentlib "github.com/easel/fizeau"
16	)
17	
18	// dispatchViaResolvedConfig is the internal SD-024 dispatch seam shared by
19	// execute-bead's worker and the post-merge reviewer. It resolves how the
20	// agent invocation is executed (test-injected runner, pre-built service, or
21	// a fresh service constructed from projectRoot) and routes through
22	// executeOnService so every durable knob lands on the dispatched request
23	// exactly once.
24	//
25	// Resolution order:
26	//  1. runner (test injection seam) — used directly via runner.Run after
27	//     applying any AgentRunRuntime overrides.
28	//  2. svc (pre-built service) — used via executeOnService.
29	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
30	//     and dispatch via executeOnService.
31	//
32	// Override fields on runtime (HarnessOverride, ModelOverride,
33	// PermissionsOverride, SessionLogDirOverride) take precedence over the
34	// matching rcfg accessors when non-empty, so callers can override one knob
35	// for a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
36	func dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, svc agentlib.FizeauService, runner AgentRunner, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
37		if err := validateRuntimeRoutingPins(rcfg, runtime); err != nil {
38			return nil, err
39		}
40		if runner != nil {
41			return runner.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
42		}
43		// DDx-local harnesses (virtual, script) are not upstream service harnesses.
44		// Route them through the local Runner so DDX_VIRTUAL_RESPONSES and script
45		// directives work correctly via compare and quorum paths, not just agent run.
46		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
47		if harness == "" {
48			harness = rcfg.Harness()
49		}
50		if harness == "virtual" || harness == "script" {
51			cfg := Config{SessionLogDir: ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, "")}
52			r := NewRunner(cfg)
53			r.WorkDir = projectRoot
54			return r.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
55		}
56		if svc == nil {
57			factory := serviceRunFactory
58			if factory == nil {
59				factory = NewServiceFromWorkDir
60			}
61			built, err := factory(projectRoot)
62			if err != nil {
63				return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
64			}
65			svc = built
66		}
67		return executeOnService(ctx, svc, projectRoot, rcfg, runtime)
68	}
69	
70	func validateRuntimeRoutingPins(rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) error {
71		if !runtime.ClearRoutingPins {
72			return nil
73		}
74		pt := rcfg.Passthrough()
75		if strings.TrimSpace(pt.Harness) == "" && strings.TrimSpace(pt.Provider) == "" && strings.TrimSpace(pt.Model) == "" {
76			return nil
77		}
78		if strings.TrimSpace(runtime.HarnessOverride) != "" ||
79			strings.TrimSpace(runtime.ProviderOverride) != "" ||
80			strings.TrimSpace(runtime.ModelOverride) != "" ||
81			strings.TrimSpace(runtime.ProfileOverride) != "" {
82			return nil
83		}
84		return fmt.Errorf("agent: lifecycle dispatch cannot clear pinned routing without an explicit replacement route")
85	}
86	
87	// buildRunArgsFromConfig assembles a RunArgs value for the test
88	// injection runner path. It applies AgentRunRuntime overrides so a caller
89	// (execute-bead worker, post-merge reviewer) can pin one durable knob for
90	// a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
91	func buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) RunArgs {
92		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
93		if harness == "" && !runtime.ClearRoutingPins {
94			harness = rcfg.Harness()
95		}
96		model := runtime.ModelOverride
97		if model == "" && !runtime.ClearRoutingPins {
98			model = rcfg.Model()
99		}
100		provider := runtime.ProviderOverride
101		if provider == "" && !runtime.ClearRoutingPins {
102			provider = rcfg.Provider()
103		}
104		permissions := runtime.PermissionsOverride
105		if permissions == "" {
106			permissions = rcfg.Permissions()
107		}
108		sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
109		if sessionLogDir == "" {
110			sessionLogDir = rcfg.SessionLogDir()
111		}
112	
113		var opts RunArgs
114		opts.Context = ctx
115		opts.Harness = harness
116		// evidence:allow-unbounded reason="caller is responsible for bounding the prompt before invoking dispatchViaResolvedConfig; downstream executeOnService hits readPromptFileBounded for PromptFile inputs"
117		opts.Prompt = runtime.Prompt
118		opts.PromptFile = runtime.PromptFile
119		opts.PromptSource = runtime.PromptSource
120		opts.Correlation = runtime.Correlation
121		opts.Role = runtime.Role
122		opts.CorrelationID = runtime.CorrelationID
123		opts.Env = runtime.Env
124		opts.Model = model
125		opts.Provider = provider
126		opts.Effort = rcfg.Effort()
127		opts.Timeout = rcfg.Timeout()
128		opts.WallClock = rcfg.WallClock()
129		opts.WorkDir = runtime.WorkDir
130		opts.Permissions = permissions
131		opts.SessionLogDir = sessionLogDir
132		return opts

1	package work
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"io"
6		"os"
7		"sync"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/workerstatus"
11	)
12	
13	// LivenessReporter observes worker liveness ticks. WithHeartbeat invokes
14	// OnTick after each TouchClaimHeartbeat so the caller can mirror the same
15	// signal to a worker-status sidecar and to the optional server probe
16	// without rewriting the bead tracker.
17	type LivenessReporter interface {
18		OnTick(now time.Time)
19	}
20	
21	// SidecarLivenessReporter is the production LivenessReporter. Each tick it
22	// (a) writes a status.json sidecar under .ddx/workers/<worker-id>/ via the
23	// workerstatus package, and (b) emits a worker.heartbeat envelope to sink
24	// (typically the loop EventSink, which is teed to the workerprobe). The
25	// sidecar write is best-effort and never fails the loop; the sink emit is
26	// best-effort and never blocks the heartbeat goroutine.
27	type SidecarLivenessReporter struct {
28		mu          sync.Mutex
29		projectRoot string
30		rec         workerstatus.LivenessRecord
31		sink        io.Writer
32		sessionID   string
33		childProbe  func(route, harness, phase string) []workerstatus.ProviderChild
34	}
35	
36	// NewSidecarLivenessReporter constructs a SidecarLivenessReporter for the
37	// given worker. projectRoot must be the project's git root (where .ddx/
38	// lives). sink may be nil for callers that don't want event mirroring.
39	func NewSidecarLivenessReporter(projectRoot, workerID, sessionID string, sink io.Writer) *SidecarLivenessReporter {
40		return &SidecarLivenessReporter{
41			projectRoot: projectRoot,
42			sink:        sink,
43			sessionID:   sessionID,
44			rec: workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
45				WorkerID:    workerID,
46				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
47				PID:         os.Getpid(),
48				StartedAt:   time.Now().UTC(),
49			},
50		}
51	}
52	
53	// SetAttempt records the in-flight attempt identity. Called once per claim
54	// before WithHeartbeat starts so the first tick records the right bead.
55	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) SetAttempt(beadID, attemptID, phase, route, harness, model, profile string, childPID int) {
56		if r == nil {
57			return
58		}
59		r.mu.Lock()
60		r.rec.CurrentBead = beadID
61		r.rec.AttemptID = attemptID
62		r.rec.Phase = phase
63		r.rec.Message = ""
64		r.rec.Route = route
65		r.rec.Harness = harness
66		r.rec.Model = model
67		r.rec.Profile = profile
68		r.rec.ChildPID = childPID
69		r.mu.Unlock()
70	}
71	
72	// SetChildProbe installs a best-effort probe invoked on each heartbeat tick.
73	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) SetChildProbe(probe func(route, harness, phase string) []workerstatus.ProviderChild) {
74		if r == nil {
75			return
76		}
77		r.mu.Lock()
78		r.childProbe = probe
79		r.mu.Unlock()
80	}
81	
82	// SetWorkerState records a non-attempt worker state in the sidecar.
83	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) SetWorkerState(phase, message string) {
84		if r == nil {
85			return
86		}
87		r.mu.Lock()
88		r.rec.CurrentBead = ""
89		r.rec.AttemptID = ""
90		r.rec.Phase = phase
91		r.rec.Message = message
92		r.rec.Route = ""
93		r.rec.Harness = ""
94		r.rec.Model = ""
95		r.rec.Profile = ""
96		r.rec.ChildPID = 0
97		r.rec.ProviderChildren = nil
98		r.mu.Unlock()
99	}
100	
101	// UpdateRoute overwrites the in-flight route fields once fizeau resolves them.
102	// Guarded on r.rec.CurrentBead == beadID; a mis-matched beadID is a no-op.
103	// Empty args do not blank existing values.
104	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) UpdateRoute(beadID, harness, model, route string) {
105		if r == nil {
106			return
107		}
108		r.mu.Lock()
109		defer r.mu.Unlock()
110		if r.rec.CurrentBead != beadID {
111			return
112		}
113		if harness != "" {
114			r.rec.Harness = harness
115		}
116		if model != "" {
117			r.rec.Model = model
118		}
119		if route != "" {
120			r.rec.Route = route
121		}
122	}
123	
124	// ClearAttempt drops the in-flight attempt identity once the attempt
125	// terminates. The worker_id and project_root remain so an idle drain loop
126	// still presents as the same worker between attempts.
127	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) ClearAttempt() {
128		if r == nil {
129			return
130		}
131		r.mu.Lock()
132		r.rec.CurrentBead = ""
133		r.rec.AttemptID = ""
134		r.rec.Phase = ""
135		r.rec.Message = ""
136		r.rec.Route = ""
137		r.rec.Harness = ""
138		r.rec.Model = ""
139		r.rec.Profile = ""
140		r.rec.ChildPID = 0
141		r.rec.ProviderChildren = nil
142		r.mu.Unlock()
143	}
144	
145	// OnTick updates the sidecar with the new last_activity_at and mirrors a
146	// worker.heartbeat envelope to the configured sink (best-effort).
147	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) OnTick(now time.Time) {
148		if r == nil {
149			return
150		}
151		r.mu.Lock()
152		probe := r.childProbe
153		route := r.rec.Route
154		harness := r.rec.Harness
155		phase := r.rec.Phase
156		hasAttempt := r.rec.CurrentBead != ""
157		r.mu.Unlock()
158	
159		var children []workerstatus.ProviderChild
160		if probe != nil && hasAttempt {
161			children = probe(route, harness, phase)
162		}
163	
164		r.mu.Lock()
165		r.rec.LastActivityAt = now.UTC()
166		r.rec.ProviderChildren = children
167		snapshot := r.rec
168		r.mu.Unlock()
169		_ = workerstatus.WriteLiveness(r.projectRoot, snapshot.WorkerID, snapshot)
170		r.emit(snapshot, now)
171	}
172	
173	// Snapshot returns a copy of the current sidecar record.
174	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) Snapshot() workerstatus.LivenessRecord {
175		r.mu.Lock()
176		defer r.mu.Unlock()
177		return r.rec
178	}
179	
180	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) emit(rec workerstatus.LivenessRecord, now time.Time) {
181		if r.sink == nil {
182			return
183		}
184		entry := map[string]any{
185			"session_id": r.sessionID,
186			"type":       "worker.heartbeat",
187			"ts":         now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
188			"data": map[string]any{
189				"worker_id":        rec.WorkerID,
190				"bead_id":          rec.CurrentBead,
191				"attempt_id":       rec.AttemptID,
192				"phase":            rec.Phase,
193				"message":          rec.Message,
194				"route":            rec.Route,
195				"harness":          rec.Harness,
196				"model":            rec.Model,
197				"profile":          rec.Profile,
198				"last_activity_at": rec.LastActivityAt.Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
199			},
200		}
201		line, err := json.Marshal(entry)
202		if err != nil {
203			return
204		}
205		line = append(line, '\n')
206		_, _ = r.sink.Write(line)
207	}

1	package work
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6	)
7	
8	// Phase is the typed work phase vocabulary.
9	type Phase string
10	
11	const (
12		PhaseQueueing Phase = "queueing"
13		PhaseRunning  Phase = "running"
14		PhaseTerminal Phase = "terminal"
15	)
16	
17	// Outcome carries the state needed to render the terminal phase/result pair.
18	// The execute loop populates the fields it already knows; the phase helper
19	// forwards them to the store so tests can verify the transition contract.
20	type Outcome struct {
21		AttemptID          string
22		Status             string
23		Detail             string
24		SessionID          string
25		ResultRev          string
26		BaseRev            string
27		PreserveRef        string
28		NoChangesRationale string
29		Disposition        string
30		ParkReason         string
31		DurationMS         int64
32	}
33	
34	// PhaseStore receives the phase transition emissions.
35	type PhaseStore interface {
36		EmitProgress(ctx context.Context, beadID string, phase Phase, outcome Outcome) error
37		EmitResult(ctx context.Context, beadID string, outcome Outcome) error
38	}
39	
40	// emitPhase centralises the phase-state transition contract so callers only
41	// need to name the phase. Terminal transitions emit both the terminal phase
42	// record and the durable bead.result record.
43	func emitPhase(ctx context.Context, store PhaseStore, beadID string, phase Phase, outcome Outcome) error {
44		if store == nil {
45			return nil
46		}
47		switch phase {
48		case PhaseQueueing, PhaseRunning:
49			return store.EmitProgress(ctx, beadID, phase, outcome)
50		case PhaseTerminal:
51			if err := store.EmitProgress(ctx, beadID, phase, outcome); err != nil {
52				return err
53			}
54			return store.EmitResult(ctx, beadID, outcome)
55		default:
56			return fmt.Errorf("unknown phase %q", phase)
57		}
58	}
59	
60	// EmitPhase is the exported wrapper used by the agent package.
61	func EmitPhase(ctx context.Context, store PhaseStore, beadID string, phase Phase, outcome Outcome) error {
62		return emitPhase(ctx, store, beadID, phase, outcome)
63	}

30	// ExecuteBeadResult captures the outcome of an execute-bead worker run.
31	// The worker populates the task-level fields (BeadID through UsageFile).
32	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult) populates the landing fields
33	// (Outcome, Status, Detail, Reason, PreserveRef, GateResults, RequiredExecSummary,
34	// ChecksFile) via ApplyLandingToResult before the result is written to disk or
35	// returned to a caller.
36	type ExecuteBeadResult struct {
37		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
38		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
39		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
40		BaseRev   string `json:"base_rev"`
41		ResultRev string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
42		// ImplementationRev is the worker's own commit SHA — the candidate revision
43		// produced by the agent before landing. Set from ResultRev by
44		// ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult before ResultRev is rewritten to the
45		// branch tip. Never overwritten after first assignment.
46		ImplementationRev string `json:"implementation_rev,omitempty"`
47		// LandedRev is the target branch tip after the coordinator fast-forwards or
48		// merges the implementation commit. Populated by ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult.
49		LandedRev string `json:"landed_rev,omitempty"`
50		// TargetBranch is the resolved branch that received the landing. It uses the
51		// landed_branch JSON name because callers care about the post-land location.
52		TargetBranch string `json:"landed_branch,omitempty"`
53		// EvidenceRev is the SHA of the trailing evidence commit when it differs from
54		// ImplementationRev. Populated by the evidence committer; empty otherwise.
55		EvidenceRev string `json:"evidence_rev,omitempty"`
56	
57		// Outcome and Status are initially set by the worker to task-level values
58		// (task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes), then overwritten by
59		// ApplyLandingToResult with the landing decision (merged / preserved /
60		// no-changes) so callers see a unified record.
61		Outcome string `json:"outcome"`
62		Status  string `json:"status,omitempty"`
63		Detail  string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
64		// OrchestratorStatus is populated after the landing/pre-merge orchestration
65		// decision so result.json can distinguish worker success from final
66		// non-landing outcomes.

68	
69		// Landing fields — populated by ApplyLandingToResult, not by ExecuteBead.

71		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
72		// CandidateRef is the project-root git ref pinned before checks and review.

75		// CycleIndex is the zero-based repair-cycle index for this candidate.

79		ChecksFile          string            `json:"checks_file,omitempty"`

82		// consumers use these to distinguish ratchet-preserved attempts from

84		RatchetEvidence []RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet_evidence,omitempty"`
85		RatchetSummary  string            `json:"ratchet_summary,omitempty"`
86	
87		// NoChangesRationale is populated when the agent wrote a rationale file to
88		// the execution bundle dir inside the worktree. It carries the agent's
89		// explanation of why no commits were made, and is preserved even when a

91		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
92	
93		// NoEvidencePaths names worktree paths that remained dirty when the agent
94		// exited without creating a commit or no_changes_rationale.txt. It helps

97	
98		// CycleTrace records the append-only execution cycle trace in order.

101	
102		// ResourceExhausted captures the root and cleanup summary when execution

11	// PushFailedReasonPrefix is the canonical Reason prefix written by the
12	// landing layer when `git push` rejects the new tip after a local merge or
13	// fast-forward succeeded. ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus matches on this prefix
14	// to map a merged-locally-but-not-pushed outcome to push_failed instead of
15	// success, so the bead stays open.
16	const PushFailedReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push failed:"
17	
18	// PushConflictReasonPrefix marks a more specific failure mode than
19	// PushFailedReasonPrefix: the local push was rejected because the remote
20	// advanced, the loop attempted an automatic pull/merge to recover, and the
21	// merge produced a conflict the loop cannot resolve. The bead is parked for
22	// human review with a short cooldown rather than a year-long park.
23	const PushConflictReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push conflict:"

25	// FailureMode constants classify *why* an execution did not land cleanly.
26	// They are orthogonal to Outcome/Status: a single failed attempt carries one
27	// outcome (e.g. preserved) and one failure mode (e.g. merge_conflict) so
28	// measurement surfaces can aggregate failures by cause.
29	const (
30		FailureModeContextOverflow                = "context_overflow"

33		FailureModeBuildFailure                   = "build_failure"
34		FailureModeTimeout                        = "timeout"

36		FailureModeNoChanges                      = "no_changes"
37		FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced             = "no_evidence_produced"
38		FailureModeRatchetMiss                    = "ratchet_miss"
39		FailureModeNoViableProvider               = "no_viable_provider"
40		FailureModeProviderConnectivity           = "provider_connectivity"
41		FailureModeServerUnavailable              = "server_unavailable"
42		FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled            = "harness_not_installed"
43		FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint = "blocked_by_passthrough_constraint"
44		FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied          = "agent_power_unsatisfied"
45		FailureModeLockContention                 = "lock_contention"
46		FailureModeWorktreeLost                   = "worktree_lost"
47		// FailureModeRouteResolutionTimeout classifies an attempt that was parked
48		// because route resolution / routing preflight exceeded the configured
49		// route-resolution timeout. The lease is released and the bead is flagged
50		// for operator attention rather than auto-retried (ddx-d8970a7b).
51		FailureModeRouteResolutionTimeout = "route_resolution_timeout"
52		// FailureModeProgressWatchdog classifies an attempt the progress watchdog
53		// terminated because phase-empty heartbeats (harness/model/route all empty)
54		// persisted past the phase budget. The worker appeared fresh to the

56		// and the bead is flagged for operator attention (ddx-dc23f001, parent
57		// ddx-8f2e0ebf criterion B).

59		// FailureModeConsecutiveWedge classifies a bead the consecutive-wedge guard
60		// stopped re-claiming because it wedged (route_resolution_timeout or
61		// progress_watchdog) on consecutive claims up to the threshold. The bead is
62		// parked to proposed for operator attention so the single-threaded worker

242	// containsAny reports whether s contains any of the given substrings. s is
243	// assumed to already be lower-cased by the caller.
244	func containsAny(s string, needles ...string) bool {
245		for _, n := range needles {
246			if n == "" {
247				continue
248			}
249			if strings.Contains(s, n) {
250				return true
251			}
252		}
253		return false
254	}

256	// classifyLandingFailureMode computes the final failure_mode for a result
257	// after the orchestrator has made its landing decision. It prefers
258	// landing-specific signals (merge conflict, gate failure) over the worker's
259	// classification and falls back to workerMode for worker-level failures
260	// (agent execution failed, context overflow, etc.). Returns "" when the
261	// bead merged cleanly.
262	func classifyLandingFailureMode(landingOutcome, landingReason string, gateResults []GateCheckResult, workerMode string) string {
263		switch landingOutcome {
264		case "merged":
265			return ""
266		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
267			return FailureModeNoChanges
268		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
269			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
270		case "error":
271			if workerMode != "" {
272				return workerMode
273			}
274			return FailureModeUnknown
275		case "preserved":
276			switch {
277			case landingReason == "merge conflict" || landingReason == "merge failed" || landingReason == "ff-merge not possible":
278				return FailureModeMergeConflict
279			case landingReason == "post-run checks failed", strings.HasPrefix(landingReason, PreMergeChecksReason):
280				return classifyGateFailure(gateResults)
281			case landingReason == RatchetPreserveReason:
282				return FailureModeRatchetMiss
283			case landingReason == AttemptIntegrityPreserveReason:
284				return FailureModeAttemptIntegrity
285			case landingReason == OperatorCancelReason:
286				return ""
287			}
288			// Other preserved reasons (e.g. "agent execution failed",
289			// "--no-merge specified") defer to the worker's classification.
290			if workerMode != "" {
291				return workerMode
292			}
293			return FailureModeUnknown
294		}
295		if workerMode != "" {
296			return workerMode
297		}
298		return FailureModeUnknown
299	}
300	
301	// classifyGateFailure inspects failed gate results to distinguish
302	// build_failure from test_failure. Gate definitions that fail to compile
303	// before running (go build, tsc, cargo build) typically surface build
304	// diagnostics in stderr; test runners surface test failure markers. When
305	// ambiguous, defaults to test_failure since the post-run checks gate is
306	// test-oriented by convention.
307	func classifyGateFailure(gateResults []GateCheckResult) string {
308		for _, gr := range gateResults {
309			if gr.Status != "fail" {
310				continue
311			}
312			combined := strings.ToLower(gr.Stdout + "\n" + gr.Stderr)
313			if containsAny(combined,
314				"build failed",
315				"compilation failed",
316				"compile error",
317				"compilation error",
318				"cannot find package",
319				"undefined:",
320				"undefined reference",
321				"syntax error") {
322				return FailureModeBuildFailure
323			}
324		}
325		return FailureModeTestFailure
326	}

13	// LivenessReporter observes worker liveness ticks. WithHeartbeat invokes
14	// OnTick after each TouchClaimHeartbeat so the caller can mirror the same
15	// signal to a worker-status sidecar and to the optional server probe
16	// without rewriting the bead tracker.
17	type LivenessReporter interface {
18		OnTick(now time.Time)
19	}
20	
21	// SidecarLivenessReporter is the production LivenessReporter. Each tick it
22	// (a) writes a status.json sidecar under .ddx/workers/<worker-id>/ via the
23	// workerstatus package, and (b) emits a worker.heartbeat envelope to sink
24	// (typically the loop EventSink, which is teed to the workerprobe). The
25	// sidecar write is best-effort and never fails the loop; the sink emit is
26	// best-effort and never blocks the heartbeat goroutine.
27	type SidecarLivenessReporter struct {
28		mu          sync.Mutex
29		projectRoot string
30		rec         workerstatus.LivenessRecord
31		sink        io.Writer
32		sessionID   string
33		childProbe  func(route, harness, phase string) []workerstatus.ProviderChild
34	}
35	
36	// NewSidecarLivenessReporter constructs a SidecarLivenessReporter for the
37	// given worker. projectRoot must be the project's git root (where .ddx/
38	// lives). sink may be nil for callers that don't want event mirroring.
39	func NewSidecarLivenessReporter(projectRoot, workerID, sessionID string, sink io.Writer) *SidecarLivenessReporter {
40		return &SidecarLivenessReporter{
41			projectRoot: projectRoot,
42			sink:        sink,
43			sessionID:   sessionID,
44			rec: workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
45				WorkerID:    workerID,
46				ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
47				PID:         os.Getpid(),
48				StartedAt:   time.Now().UTC(),
49			},
50		}
51	}
52	
53	// SetAttempt records the in-flight attempt identity. Called once per claim
54	// before WithHeartbeat starts so the first tick records the right bead.
55	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) SetAttempt(beadID, attemptID, phase, route, harness, model, profile string, childPID int) {
56		if r == nil {
57			return
58		}
59		r.mu.Lock()
60		r.rec.CurrentBead = beadID
61		r.rec.AttemptID = attemptID
62		r.rec.Phase = phase
63		r.rec.Message = ""
64		r.rec.Route = route
65		r.rec.Harness = harness
66		r.rec.Model = model
67		r.rec.Profile = profile
68		r.rec.ChildPID = childPID
69		r.mu.Unlock()
70	}
71	
72	// SetChildProbe installs a best-effort probe invoked on each heartbeat tick.
73	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) SetChildProbe(probe func(route, harness, phase string) []workerstatus.ProviderChild) {
74		if r == nil {
75			return
76		}
77		r.mu.Lock()
78		r.childProbe = probe
79		r.mu.Unlock()
80	}
81	
82	// SetWorkerState records a non-attempt worker state in the sidecar.
83	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) SetWorkerState(phase, message string) {
84		if r == nil {
85			return
86		}
87		r.mu.Lock()
88		r.rec.CurrentBead = ""
89		r.rec.AttemptID = ""
90		r.rec.Phase = phase
91		r.rec.Message = message
92		r.rec.Route = ""
93		r.rec.Harness = ""
94		r.rec.Model = ""
95		r.rec.Profile = ""
96		r.rec.ChildPID = 0
97		r.rec.ProviderChildren = nil
98		r.mu.Unlock()
99	}
100	
101	// UpdateRoute overwrites the in-flight route fields once fizeau resolves them.
102	// Guarded on r.rec.CurrentBead == beadID; a mis-matched beadID is a no-op.
103	// Empty args do not blank existing values.
104	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) UpdateRoute(beadID, harness, model, route string) {
105		if r == nil {
106			return
107		}
108		r.mu.Lock()
109		defer r.mu.Unlock()
110		if r.rec.CurrentBead != beadID {
111			return
112		}
113		if harness != "" {
114			r.rec.Harness = harness
115		}
116		if model != "" {
117			r.rec.Model = model
118		}
119		if route != "" {
120			r.rec.Route = route
121		}
122	}
123	
124	// ClearAttempt drops the in-flight attempt identity once the attempt
125	// terminates. The worker_id and project_root remain so an idle drain loop
126	// still presents as the same worker between attempts.
127	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) ClearAttempt() {
128		if r == nil {
129			return
130		}
131		r.mu.Lock()
132		r.rec.CurrentBead = ""
133		r.rec.AttemptID = ""
134		r.rec.Phase = ""
135		r.rec.Message = ""
136		r.rec.Route = ""
137		r.rec.Harness = ""
138		r.rec.Model = ""
139		r.rec.Profile = ""
140		r.rec.ChildPID = 0
141		r.rec.ProviderChildren = nil
142		r.mu.Unlock()
143	}
144	
145	// OnTick updates the sidecar with the new last_activity_at and mirrors a
146	// worker.heartbeat envelope to the configured sink (best-effort).
147	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) OnTick(now time.Time) {
148		if r == nil {
149			return
150		}
151		r.mu.Lock()
152		probe := r.childProbe
153		route := r.rec.Route
154		harness := r.rec.Harness
155		phase := r.rec.Phase
156		hasAttempt := r.rec.CurrentBead != ""
157		r.mu.Unlock()
158	
159		var children []workerstatus.ProviderChild
160		if probe != nil && hasAttempt {
161			children = probe(route, harness, phase)
162		}
163	
164		r.mu.Lock()
165		r.rec.LastActivityAt = now.UTC()
166		r.rec.ProviderChildren = children
167		snapshot := r.rec
168		r.mu.Unlock()
169		_ = workerstatus.WriteLiveness(r.projectRoot, snapshot.WorkerID, snapshot)
170		r.emit(snapshot, now)
171	}
172	
173	// Snapshot returns a copy of the current sidecar record.
174	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) Snapshot() workerstatus.LivenessRecord {
175		r.mu.Lock()
176		defer r.mu.Unlock()
177		return r.rec
178	}
179	
180	func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) emit(rec workerstatus.LivenessRecord, now time.Time) {
181		if r.sink == nil {
182			return
183		}
184		entry := map[string]any{
185			"session_id": r.sessionID,
186			"type":       "worker.heartbeat",
187			"ts":         now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
188			"data": map[string]any{
189				"worker_id":        rec.WorkerID,
190				"bead_id":          rec.CurrentBead,
191				"attempt_id":       rec.AttemptID,
192				"phase":            rec.Phase,
193				"message":          rec.Message,
194				"route":            rec.Route,
195				"harness":          rec.Harness,
196				"model":            rec.Model,
197				"profile":          rec.Profile,
198				"last_activity_at": rec.LastActivityAt.Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
199			},
200		}
201		line, err := json.Marshal(entry)
202		if err != nil {
203			return
204		}
205		line = append(line, '\n')
206		_, _ = r.sink.Write(line)
207	}

1	package work

3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6		"time"
7	
8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/workerstatus"
9	)
10	
11	// PhaseBudgets configures the progress watchdog's per-phase patience window.
12	// A worker that emits "phase-empty" heartbeats — harness, model, and route all
13	// empty — past the applicable budget is treated as wedged: nothing is making
14	// forward progress even though each heartbeat keeps advancing last_activity_at,
15	// so the liveness TTL alone reports the worker healthy. This is the 80-minute
16	// wedge observed in ddx-8f2e0ebf (criterion B), where phase=running heartbeats
17	// with empty harness/model/route persisted indefinitely.
18	type PhaseBudgets struct {
19		// Resolving bounds how long phase-empty heartbeats may persist before a
20		// route is resolved (no harness/model assigned yet) before the watchdog
21		// fires.
22		Resolving time.Duration
23		// Running bounds how long phase-empty heartbeats may persist once the
24		// worker has entered the running phase. It is longer than Resolving
25		// because a genuinely running attempt holds the phase=running marker for
26		// many minutes.
27		Running time.Duration
28	}
29	
30	// DefaultPhaseBudgets returns the watchdog's documented default budgets:
31	// 5 minutes while resolving a route and 30 minutes once running.
32	func DefaultPhaseBudgets() PhaseBudgets {
33		return PhaseBudgets{
34			Resolving: 5 * time.Minute,
35			Running:   30 * time.Minute,
36		}
37	}
38	
39	// budgetForPhase selects the budget that applies to the given phase. The
40	// running phase uses the longer Running budget; every other phase (queueing,
41	// terminal, or empty) uses Resolving.
42	func (b PhaseBudgets) budgetForPhase(phase string) time.Duration {
43		if phase == string(PhaseRunning) {
44			return b.Running
45		}
46		return b.Resolving
47	}
48	
49	// phaseEmpty reports the wedge signature: harness, model, and route all empty.
50	func phaseEmpty(harness, model, route string) bool {
51		return strings.TrimSpace(harness) == "" &&
52			strings.TrimSpace(model) == "" &&
53			strings.TrimSpace(route) == ""
54	}
55	
56	// progressWatchdog tracks how long the phase-empty signature has persisted and
57	// reports when it crosses the phase budget.
58	type progressWatchdog struct {
59		budgets    PhaseBudgets
60		emptySince time.Time
61		emptyPhase string
62	}
63	
64	// observe records one heartbeat snapshot. It reports whether the phase-empty
65	// signature (harness, model, and route all empty) has now persisted at or
66	// beyond the applicable phase budget, along with the budget that applied. A
67	// heartbeat carrying any non-empty route field resets the phase-empty timer:
68	// the worker made forward progress.
69	func (w *progressWatchdog) observe(now time.Time, phase, harness, model, route string) (fired bool, budget time.Duration) {
70		if !phaseEmpty(harness, model, route) {
71			w.emptySince = time.Time{}
72			w.emptyPhase = ""
73			return false, 0
74		}
75		if w.emptySince.IsZero() {
76			w.emptySince = now
77			w.emptyPhase = phase
78		}
79		budget = w.budgets.budgetForPhase(w.emptyPhase)
80		return now.Sub(w.emptySince) >= budget, budget
81	}
82	
83	// ProgressWatchdogConfig wires the phase-empty progress watchdog. The watchdog
84	// runs as a goroutine for the lifetime of an attempt, alongside WithHeartbeat:
85	// each tick it inspects the liveness snapshot and, when the phase-empty
86	// signature has persisted past the phase budget, it invokes OnWedged so the
87	// caller can cancel the attempt, release the lease, and flag the wedge for
88	// operator attention.
89	type ProgressWatchdogConfig struct {
90		Budgets  PhaseBudgets
91		Snapshot func() workerstatus.LivenessRecord
92		Now      func() time.Time
93		// OnWedged is invoked exactly once, with the snapshot that tripped the
94		// watchdog and the budget it exceeded, when the phase-empty signature
95		// persists past the budget.
96		OnWedged func(rec workerstatus.LivenessRecord, budget time.Duration)
97	}
98	
99	// RunProgressWatchdog runs the phase-empty progress watchdog until ctx is
100	// cancelled or the watchdog fires. On fire it invokes cfg.OnWedged once and

func (r *SidecarLivenessReporter) ClearAttempt() {
	if r == nil {
		return
	}

## 3. Timeout/Wall-Clock/Watchdog Around Dispatch

### No Direct context.WithTimeout/WithDeadline Before Dispatch
- **File**: `/home/erik/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-f2b7cf89-20260702T122209-5ff98ac0/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`
- **Line 1115**: Dispatch context created with only `context.WithCancel`:
  ```go
  dispatchCtx, dispatchCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
  ```
- **No timeout/deadline applied at dispatch call site**

### Operator-Initiated Cancellation (Only Explicit Deadline)
- **File**: `/home/erik/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-f2b7cf89-20260702T122209-5ff98ac0/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`
- **Lines 1110-1117**: Cancel poll mechanism
  ```go
  // Operator-cancel mid-attempt poll (ADR-022 §Cancel SLA). The poll
  // re-reads the bead's Extra every CancelPollInterval; on
  // cancel-requested:true it cancels the dispatch context
  dispatchCtx, dispatchCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
  defer dispatchCancel()
  cancelHonored := startCancelPoll(dispatchCtx, dispatchCancel, beadID, runtime.BeadCancel)
  ```
- This is **not** a wall-clock timeout; it's an operator-driven cancellation via bead metadata polling

### Progress Watchdog (Detects Wedges, Not Hard Timeout)
- **File**: `/home/erik/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-f2b7cf89-20260702T122209-5ff98ac0/cli/internal/agent/work/watchdog.go`
- **Lines 30-36**: Budgets (not hard timeouts)
  ```go
  func DefaultPhaseBudgets() PhaseBudgets {
      return PhaseBudgets{
          Resolving: 5 * time.Minute,
          Running:   30 * time.Minute,
      }
  }
  ```
- **Lines 64-81**: observe() method detects when phase-empty heartbeats persist past budget
  - This fires when **no forward progress** is detected, not after elapsed wall-clock time
  - Different from a traditional timeout

### Watchdog Firing Cancels Dispatch
- **File**: `/home/erik/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-f2b7cf89-20260702T122209-5ff98ac0/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`
- **Lines 3305-3320**: Watchdog goroutine (launched in parallel with dispatch)
  - Calls `attemptCancel()` when wedged
  - This cancels `dispatchCtx`, which terminates `attemptBackend.Run()`

### Conclusion on Timeout for attemptBackend.Run()
- **The dispatch has NO hard wall-clock timeout**
- **The dispatch has NO context.WithTimeout or context.WithDeadline**
- **The only cancellation mechanisms are**:
  1. Operator cancel (via bead metadata polling)
  2. Progress watchdog (detects no-forward-progress over 5-30 min windows, not absolute timeout)
  3. External close watcher (parallel attempt closed the bead)
- **If attemptBackend.Run never returns and these guards don't fire, the dispatch blocks indefinitely**

1	package work
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"sync"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	type heartbeatStore interface {
10		TouchClaimHeartbeat(string) error
11	}
12	
13	// WithHeartbeat runs fn while periodically calling store.TouchClaimHeartbeat
14	// on a background goroutine at the given interval. When reporter is non-nil,
15	// reporter.OnTick is invoked after each TouchClaimHeartbeat so callers can
16	// mirror the same liveness signal to a worker-status sidecar and the
17	// optional server probe without rewriting the bead tracker. The goroutine
18	// is stopped and waited for before WithHeartbeat returns.
19	func WithHeartbeat[T any](ctx context.Context, beadID string, interval time.Duration, store heartbeatStore, reporter LivenessReporter, fn func() (T, error)) (T, error) {
20		ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
21		defer ticker.Stop()
22		return withHeartbeatCh(ctx, beadID, ticker.C, store, reporter, fn)
23	}
24	
25	// withHeartbeatCh is the injectable variant used by tests to supply a stub tick channel.
26	func withHeartbeatCh[T any](ctx context.Context, beadID string, tickCh <-chan time.Time, store heartbeatStore, reporter LivenessReporter, fn func() (T, error)) (T, error) {
27		hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
28		var wg sync.WaitGroup
29		wg.Add(1)
30		go func() {
31			defer wg.Done()
32			for {
33				select {
34				case <-hbCtx.Done():
35					return
36				case tick := <-tickCh:
37					_ = store.TouchClaimHeartbeat(beadID)
38					if reporter != nil {
39						reporter.OnTick(tick)
40					}
41				}
42			}
43		}()
44		result, err := fn()
45		hbCancel()
46		wg.Wait()
47		return result, err
48	}

23	// TestWorkLoop_ClaimAndHeartbeatDoNotMutateTrackerBeforeOutcome guards the
24	// ddx-092c4343 contract: a long-running inline ExecuteBeadWorker.Run with a
25	// short heartbeat interval must refresh .ddx/workers/<worker-id>/status.json
26	// last_activity_at on every tick, while leaving the .ddx/beads.jsonl byte
27	// content untouched from before claim acquisition through the terminal outcome
28	// write. Live worker claim state belongs in sidecars, not in tracked rows.
29	func TestWorkLoop_ClaimAndHeartbeatDoNotMutateTrackerBeforeOutcome(t *testing.T) {
30		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
31		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ddxroot.DirName)
32		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
33	
34		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
35		require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))
36		target := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-liveness-1", Title: "Liveness sidecar bead"}
37		require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), target))
38	
39		beadsPath := filepath.Join(ddxDir, "beads.jsonl")
40		beforeClaimSnapshot, err := os.ReadFile(beadsPath)
41		require.NoError(t, err)
42		sessionID := "sess-liveness-test"
43	
44		var (
45			mu                                sync.Mutex
46			claimSnapshot, preOutcomeSnapshot []byte
47			observedTimestamps                []time.Time
48			execErr                           error
49		)
50	
51		executor := ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
52			// Snapshot beads.jsonl right after the executor was handed control,
53			// which is immediately after the claim write.
54			snap, err := os.ReadFile(beadsPath)
55			if err != nil {
56				execErr = err
57				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, err
58			}
59			mu.Lock()
60			claimSnapshot = snap
61			mu.Unlock()
62	
63			// Observe the sidecar advancing across ticks. With a 5ms heartbeat
64			// interval, 80ms of wall-clock gives ~16 ticks; we only require 3
65			// distinct timestamps to confirm "every tick" advances last_activity_at
66			// without making the test flaky on slow CI.
67			seen := map[time.Time]struct{}{}
68			deadline := time.Now().Add(500 * time.Millisecond)
69			for time.Now().Before(deadline) && len(seen) < 3 {
70				rec, rerr := workerstatus.ReadLiveness(projectRoot, sessionID)
71				if rerr == nil && !rec.LastActivityAt.IsZero() {
72					if _, ok := seen[rec.LastActivityAt]; !ok {
73						seen[rec.LastActivityAt] = struct{}{}
74						mu.Lock()
75						observedTimestamps = append(observedTimestamps, rec.LastActivityAt)
76						mu.Unlock()
77					}
78				}
79				time.Sleep(8 * time.Millisecond)
80			}
81	
82			// Snapshot beads.jsonl right before returning the outcome — this is
83			// the byte state immediately before the loop performs the terminal
84			// CloseWithEvidence / Unclaim write.
85			snap2, err := os.ReadFile(beadsPath)
86			if err != nil {
87				execErr = err
88				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, err
89			}
90			mu.Lock()
91			preOutcomeSnapshot = snap2
92			mu.Unlock()
93	
94			return ExecuteBeadReport{
95				BeadID:    beadID,
96				Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
97				SessionID: sessionID,
98				ResultRev: "live-rev",
99				BaseRev:   "base-rev",
100			}, nil
101		})
102	
103		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{Store: store, Executor: executor}
104	
105		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{
106			Assignee:          "worker",
107			HeartbeatInterval: 5 * time.Millisecond,
108		}
109		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
110	
111		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
112			Mode:        executeloop.ModeOnce,
113			ProjectRoot: projectRoot,
114			SessionID:   sessionID,
115			WorkerID:    "worker",
116		})
117		require.NoError(t, err)
118		require.NotNil(t, result)
119		require.NoError(t, execErr)
120		require.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts, "the single ready bead must be claimed and attempted")
121	
122		mu.Lock()
123		defer mu.Unlock()
124	
125		require.NotEmpty(t, claimSnapshot, "claim-time beads.jsonl snapshot must be captured")
126		require.NotEmpty(t, preOutcomeSnapshot, "pre-outcome beads.jsonl snapshot must be captured")
127		assert.True(t, bytes.Equal(beforeClaimSnapshot, claimSnapshot),
128			"beads.jsonl byte content must stay unchanged across worker claim acquisition")
129		assert.True(t, bytes.Equal(claimSnapshot, preOutcomeSnapshot),
130			"beads.jsonl byte content must not change between the claim write and the terminal outcome write; heartbeat ticks must not rewrite the tracker")
131	
132		require.GreaterOrEqualf(t, len(observedTimestamps), 3,
133			"sidecar last_activity_at must advance on every heartbeat tick (saw %d distinct timestamps)", len(observedTimestamps))
134		for i := 1; i < len(observedTimestamps); i++ {
135			assert.Truef(t, observedTimestamps[i].After(observedTimestamps[i-1]),
136				"sidecar last_activity_at must strictly advance across ticks: %v -> %v",
137				observedTimestamps[i-1], observedTimestamps[i])
138		}
139	
140		// Final sidecar must still be present after the attempt finalises.
141		finalRec, err := workerstatus.ReadLiveness(projectRoot, sessionID)
142		require.NoError(t, err)
143		assert.Equal(t, sessionID, finalRec.WorkerID)
144	}

146	func TestWorkLoop_MirrorsPeriodicLivenessWithoutTrackerHeartbeatSpam(t *testing.T) {
147		TestWorkLoop_ClaimAndHeartbeatDoNotMutateTrackerBeforeOutcome(t)
148	}

150	// TestWorkerStatusSidecar_RecordsCurrentBeadAttemptAndLastActivity guards the
151	// ddx-1be8df2b contract that after several ticks of a simulated attempt the
152	// sidecar JSON carries every operator-facing field: worker_id, current_bead,
153	// attempt_id, phase, last_activity_at, and pid (when available). Each tick
154	// must overwrite last_activity_at with the current time so an operator can
155	// answer "is this worker still alive and what is it on?" from the file alone.
156	func TestWorkerStatusSidecar_RecordsCurrentBeadAttemptAndLastActivity(t *testing.T) {
157		projectRoot := t.TempDir()
158	
159		workerID := "worker-1be8df2b"
160		rep := work.NewSidecarLivenessReporter(projectRoot, workerID, "sess-status", nil)
161		rep.SetAttempt(
162			"ddx-aabbccdd",
163			"att-20260514-001",
164			"running",
165			"balanced",
166			"claude",
167			"opus",
168			"balanced",
169			7777,
170		)
171	
172		const ticks = 5
173		var (
174			timestamps    []time.Time
175			ticksObserved int32
176		)
177		base := time.Now().UTC()
178		for i := 0; i < ticks; i++ {
179			// Drive distinct, strictly-increasing tick times so we can assert
180			// last_activity_at advances on every tick without depending on
181			// wall-clock resolution.
182			tick := base.Add(time.Duration(i+1) * time.Millisecond)
183			rep.OnTick(tick)
184			atomic.AddInt32(&ticksObserved, 1)
185	
186			rec, err := workerstatus.ReadLiveness(projectRoot, workerID)
187			require.NoError(t, err, "sidecar must be readable after tick %d", i)
188			timestamps = append(timestamps, rec.LastActivityAt)
189		}
190	
191		require.Equal(t, int32(ticks), atomic.LoadInt32(&ticksObserved))
192	
193		rec, err := workerstatus.ReadLiveness(projectRoot, workerID)
194		require.NoError(t, err)
195	
196		assert.Equal(t, workerID, rec.WorkerID, "worker_id must be persisted")
197		assert.Equal(t, "ddx-aabbccdd", rec.CurrentBead, "current_bead must be persisted")
198		assert.Equal(t, "att-20260514-001", rec.AttemptID, "attempt_id must be persisted")
199		assert.Equal(t, "running", rec.Phase, "phase must be persisted")
200		assert.Equal(t, "balanced", rec.Route)
201		assert.Equal(t, "claude", rec.Harness)
202		assert.Equal(t, "opus", rec.Model)
203		assert.Equal(t, 7777, rec.ChildPID)
204		assert.Equal(t, os.Getpid(), rec.PID, "worker pid must be recorded when available")
205		assert.False(t, rec.LastActivityAt.IsZero(), "last_activity_at must be set")
206		assert.True(t, rec.LastActivityAt.Equal(base.Add(time.Duration(ticks)*time.Millisecond)),
207			"last_activity_at must reflect the most recent tick time")
208	
209		// AC #3: writes are atomic — the file content stored on disk is a valid
210		// JSON document at every observable moment. We verify by checking that
211		// each post-tick read returned a strictly-advancing timestamp (no torn
212		// reads would have surfaced as a parse error in ReadLiveness above).
213		for i := 1; i < len(timestamps); i++ {
214			assert.Truef(t, timestamps[i].After(timestamps[i-1]),
215				"each tick must advance last_activity_at: tick %d=%v, tick %d=%v",
216				i-1, timestamps[i-1], i, timestamps[i])
217		}
218	}

3	import (
4		"context"

14	// TestWatchdogPhaseBudgetDefaults pins the documented default phase budgets so
15	// a change to the watchdog's patience is a deliberate, reviewed edit (AC #2).
16	func TestWatchdogPhaseBudgetDefaults(t *testing.T) {
17		b := DefaultPhaseBudgets()
18		assert.Equal(t, 5*time.Minute, b.Resolving, "default resolving budget must be 5m")
19		assert.Equal(t, 30*time.Minute, b.Running, "default running budget must be 30m")
20	
21		// The budget selection routes the running phase to the longer window and
22		// every other phase to the shorter resolving window.
23		assert.Equal(t, b.Running, b.budgetForPhase(string(PhaseRunning)))
24		assert.Equal(t, b.Resolving, b.budgetForPhase(string(PhaseQueueing)))
25		assert.Equal(t, b.Resolving, b.budgetForPhase(""))
26	}
27	
28	// TestProgressWatchdogObserveResetsOnProgress verifies the core observation
29	// logic: a non-empty route field resets the phase-empty timer, and the
30	// watchdog only fires once the phase-empty signature has persisted past the
31	// applicable budget.
32	func TestProgressWatchdogObserveResetsOnProgress(t *testing.T) {
33		wd := &progressWatchdog{budgets: PhaseBudgets{Resolving: time.Minute, Running: 10 * time.Minute}}
34		base := time.Date(2026, 5, 26, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
35	
36		// Phase-empty but within budget: no fire.
37		fired, budget := wd.observe(base, "running", "", "", "")
38		require.False(t, fired)
39		require.Equal(t, 10*time.Minute, budget, "phase=running selects the running budget")
40	
41		// A heartbeat that carries harness+model is forward progress: reset.
42		fired, _ = wd.observe(base.Add(20*time.Second), "running", "claude", "opus", "balanced")
43		require.False(t, fired)
44	
45		// Back to phase-empty: the timer restarts from this observation, so even
46		// well past the original start it has not yet crossed the budget.
47		fired, _ = wd.observe(base.Add(30*time.Second), "running", "", "", "")
48		require.False(t, fired)
49	
50		// Now persist phase-empty past the running budget from the restart point.
51		fired, _ = wd.observe(base.Add(30*time.Second+11*time.Minute), "running", "", "", "")
52		require.True(t, fired, "phase-empty past the running budget must fire")
53	}
54	
55	// TestRunProgressWatchdogFiresOnPhaseEmpty drives empty-field snapshots through
56	// the runner for budget+epsilon and asserts OnWedged fires once with the
57	// snapshot and budget that tripped it.
58	func TestRunProgressWatchdogFiresOnPhaseEmpty(t *testing.T) {
59		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
60		defer cancel()
61	
62		tickCh := make(chan time.Time)
63		base := time.Date(2026, 5, 26, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
64		var clockNanos atomic.Int64
65		clockNanos.Store(base.UnixNano())
66		nowFn := func() time.Time { return time.Unix(0, clockNanos.Load()).UTC() }
67	
68		var wedged workerstatus.LivenessRecord
69		var firedBudget time.Duration
70		fireCount := 0
71		done := make(chan struct{})
72	
73		go func() {
74			runProgressWatchdog(ctx, tickCh, ProgressWatchdogConfig{
75				Budgets: PhaseBudgets{Resolving: time.Minute, Running: time.Minute},
76				Now:     nowFn,
77				Snapshot: func() workerstatus.LivenessRecord {
78					return workerstatus.LivenessRecord{
79						CurrentBead:    "ddx-wedged",
80						AttemptID:      "att-wedged-001",
81						Phase:          string(PhaseRunning),
82						LastActivityAt: nowFn(),
83					}
84				},
85				OnWedged: func(rec workerstatus.LivenessRecord, budget time.Duration) {
86					// Reads here are published to the main goroutine by the
87					// close(done) below, which happens-after this callback returns.
88					fireCount++
89					wedged = rec
90					firedBudget = budget
91				},
92			})
93			close(done)
94		}()
95	
96		// First phase-empty tick at t0: starts the timer, does not fire.
97		tickCh <- nowFn()
98		// Advance the clock past the budget, then tick again: budget+epsilon.
99		clockNanos.Store(base.Add(time.Minute + time.Second).UnixNano())
100		tickCh <- nowFn()
101	
102		select {
103		case <-done:
104		case <-time.After(2 * time.Second):
105			t.Fatal("progress watchdog did not fire on persistent phase-empty heartbeats")
106		}
107	
108		assert.Equal(t, 1, fireCount, "OnWedged must fire exactly once")
109		assert.Equal(t, "ddx-wedged", wedged.CurrentBead)
110		assert.Equal(t, "att-wedged-001", wedged.AttemptID)
111		assert.Equal(t, time.Minute, firedBudget)
112	}
113	
114	// TestProgressWatchdog_PopulatedRouteNotWedged verifies AC #3: once the route
115	// fields are populated (via UpdateRoute / the onRouteResolved callback),
116	// observe returns fired=false even when running well past the Running budget.
117	func TestProgressWatchdog_PopulatedRouteNotWedged(t *testing.T) {
118		wd := &progressWatchdog{budgets: PhaseBudgets{Resolving: time.Minute, Running: 30 * time.Minute}}
119		base := time.Date(2026, 5, 26, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
120	
121		// Arm with phase-empty (route not yet known).
122		fired, _ := wd.observe(base, "running", "", "", "")
123		require.False(t, fired, "within budget: should not fire yet")
124	
125		// Route resolves: non-empty route field resets the timer.
126		fired, _ = wd.observe(base.Add(time.Minute), "running", "fiz", "sonnet-4.6", "anthropic")
127		require.False(t, fired, "populated route must reset the phase-empty timer")
128	
129		// Well past the Running budget but route is still non-empty: must NOT fire.
130		fired, _ = wd.observe(base.Add(35*time.Minute), "running", "fiz", "sonnet-4.6", "anthropic")
131		require.False(t, fired, "populated route must not be treated as a wedge regardless of elapsed time")
132	}
133	
134	// TestRunExternalCloseWatcherFiresOnClose drives the watcher with a status
135	// reader that flips to closed and asserts OnClosed fires once and the watcher
136	// returns.
137	func TestRunExternalCloseWatcherFiresOnClose(t *testing.T) {
138		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
139		defer cancel()
140	
141		tickCh := make(chan time.Time)
142		var closedNow atomic.Bool
143		var closeCount atomic.Int32
144		done := make(chan struct{})
145	
146		go func() {
147			runExternalCloseWatcher(ctx, tickCh, ExternalCloseWatcherConfig{
148				IsClosed: func() (bool, error) { return closedNow.Load(), nil },
149				OnClosed: func() { closeCount.Add(1) },
150			})
151			close(done)
152		}()
153	
154		// Not closed yet: watcher keeps polling.
155		tickCh <- time.Now()
156		// Another attempt closes the bead; next tick observes it.
157		closedNow.Store(true)
158		tickCh <- time.Now()
159	
160		select {
161		case <-done:
162		case <-time.After(2 * time.Second):
163			t.Fatal("external-close watcher did not fire when the bead transitioned to closed")
164		}
165		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), closeCount.Load(), "OnClosed must fire exactly once")
166	}

package work

import (
	"context"
	"sync/atomic"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/workerstatus"
)

func TestDebugRouteDeath(t *testing.T) {
	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	defer cancel()
	tickCh := make(chan time.Time)
	base := time.Date(2026, 7, 2, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
	var clockNanos atomic.Int64
	clockNanos.Store(base.UnixNano())
	nowFn := func() time.Time { return time.Unix(0, clockNanos.Load()).UTC() }
	var children atomic.Value
	children.Store([]workerstatus.ProviderChild{{PID: 111, Provider: "claude", RouteOwner: "claude/opus"}})
	done := make(chan struct{})
	go func() {
		runProgressWatchdog(ctx, tickCh, ProgressWatchdogConfig{
			Budgets: PhaseBudgets{Resolving: time.Hour, Running: time.Hour},
			RouteChildGrace: 20 * time.Second,
			Now: nowFn,
			Snapshot: func() workerstatus.LivenessRecord {
				return workerstatus.LivenessRecord{Phase: string(PhaseRunning), Route: "claude/opus", ProviderChildren: children.Load().([]workerstatus.ProviderChild), LastActivityAt: nowFn()}
			},
			OnRouteChildDied: func(rec workerstatus.LivenessRecord) { t.Logf("DIED fired"); },
		})
		close(done)
	}()
	t.Logf("send tick1")
	tickCh <- nowFn()
	children.Store([]workerstatus.ProviderChild{{PID: 222, Provider: "codex", NonRoute: true}})
	clockNanos.Store(base.Add(time.Second).UnixNano())
	t.Logf("send tick2")
	tickCh <- nowFn()
	clockNanos.Store(base.Add(30 * time.Second).UnixNano())
	t.Logf("send tick3")
	select {
	case tickCh <- nowFn():
		t.Logf("tick3 sent")
	case <-done:
		t.Logf("done before tick3 sent")
	}
	select {
	case <-done:
		t.Logf("done closed")
	case <-time.After(2 * time.Second):
		t.Fatal("timeout")
	}
}
EOF

# unrelated commits. Stages B–E migrate the remaining historical

    evidence-lint:

      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts)"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
